title: Encouraging Divergent Thinking in Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Debate
authors_or_maintainers: Tian Liang, Zhiwei He, Wenxiang Jiao, Xing Wang, Yan Wang, Rui Wang, Yujiu Yang, Shuming Shi, Zhaopeng Tu
summary: Identifies the 'Degeneration-of-Thought' (DoT) problem — once an LLM commits to an answer, it struggles to generate genuinely novel alternatives. Proposes Multi-Agent Debate (MAD): two affirmative/negative debaters in a 'tit-for-tat' state plus a judge. Empirically improves on commonsense MT and counter-intuitive arithmetic. Key finding: a *moderate* level of disagreement is optimal.
applicable_pattern: Spawn N agents with deliberately opposed system prompts. Each round, each sees the others' last argument and must respond. A judge agent monitors and terminates when divergence is sufficient OR convergence is reached. Tunable knob: 'tit-for-tat temperature'.
fit_for_business_ideation: After retrieving 2 concepts ('circular economy' + 'AI agents'), spawn a Bull and Bear agent who debate viability. The judge surfaces ideas only when the Bull survives substantive Bear pushback — screens out weak superficial recombinations.

title: Improving Factuality and Reasoning in Language Models through Multiagent Debate
authors_or_maintainers: Yilun Du, Shuang Li, Antonio Torralba, Joshua B. Tenenbaum, Igor Mordatch (MIT/Google Brain)
summary: N parallel LLM instances each generate an answer, then each is shown the others' answers and asked to revise. Repeated for several rounds. Reduces hallucinations and improves reasoning across six benchmarks. The 'society of minds' framing.
applicable_pattern: Parallel-then-cross-pollinate debate (vs. sequential turn-taking). Round 1: N agents generate independently. Round 2..K: each sees the full set of last-round answers and revises. Cheaper than turn-taking debate; natural ensemble diversity.
fit_for_business_ideation: Use as 'idea convergence' pass after divergent generation. Spawn 5 agents to propose business models around retrieved concepts in parallel; then run 2 rounds of cross-revision.

title: Unleashing Cognitive Synergy in LLMs: A Task-Solving Agent through Multi-Persona Self-Collaboration (Solo Performance Prompting)
authors_or_maintainers: Zhenhailong Wang, Shaoguang Mao, Wenshan Wu, Tao Ge, Furu Wei, Heng Ji
summary: Solo Performance Prompting (SPP) makes a single LLM simulate a panel of personas in one prompt. The model identifies which experts the task needs, role-plays them in sequence, with a Leader synthesizing. Cognitive synergy emerges only at GPT-4-class scale.
applicable_pattern: One-shot multi-persona self-collaboration: prompt LLM to (1) auto-generate personas needed (Marketer, Engineer, Skeptical CFO, Target User), (2) simulate dialogue, (3) Leader synthesizes. Cheap inner loop alternative to real subagents.
fit_for_business_ideation: Cheap inner ideation loop *inside* a single subagent. Get persona diversity without paying for N separate agent calls. Pair with real multi-agent debate at the outer layer.

title: Mixture-of-Agents Enhances Large Language Model Capabilities
authors_or_maintainers: Junlin Wang, Jue Wang, Ben Athiwaratkun, Ce Zhang, James Zou (Together AI / Duke / Stanford)
summary: Layered architecture: layer-1 has N proposer agents (often *different* models). Each subsequent layer's agents receive *all* layer-1 outputs as context and refine. Final aggregator emits the answer. Open-source MoA hits 65.1% on AlpacaEval 2.0 vs. GPT-4 Omni's 57.5%.
applicable_pattern: Heterogeneous proposer-aggregator stack. Layer 1 = diverse models/prompts proposing. Layer 2+ = refiners conditioning on full slate. Final = aggregator. Model-diversity is doing real work — same model with different prompts is weaker.
fit_for_business_ideation: Run idea generation across different model families (Claude, GPT-4, Llama, Mixtral) in parallel — each has different priors about 'a good business'. Aggregator picks strongest.

title: ReConcile: Round-Table Conference Improves Reasoning via Consensus among Diverse LLMs
authors_or_maintainers: Justin Chen, Swarnadeep Saha, Mohit Bansal (UNC Chapel Hill)
summary: Multi-model 'round-table' where heterogeneous LLMs (GPT-4, Claude, Bard) debate with explicit confidence scores and human-rectifying-explanation demonstrations. Final answer via confidence-weighted vote. +11.4% over single-agent and prior multi-agent baselines.
applicable_pattern: Confidence-weighted multi-model debate. Each agent emits (answer, justification, confidence ∈ [0,1]). Final aggregation = confidence-weighted vote (not majority). Good for calibrated single output.
fit_for_business_ideation: Final ranking layer. After generating a slate, run a ReConcile pass where 3 heterogeneous-model judges score on novelty/feasibility/market-fit with confidences; weighted-vote produces final ranking that's calibrated and less single-model-biased.

title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
authors_or_maintainers: Aman Madaan, Niket Tandon, Prakhar Gupta, Skyler Hallinan, et al. (CMU/Allen AI/UW/NVIDIA/Google)
summary: Single LLM plays generator → critic → refiner in a loop, no training, no labels, up to ~4 iterations. ~20% absolute improvement over single-shot generation across 7 tasks. Canonical reference for the generator-critic prompting pattern.
applicable_pattern: FEEDBACK → REFINE loop on a single agent. Generate → prompt same model with critique-specific instructions ('rate originality 1-10, list 3 weaknesses') → prompt model to revise. Stop at quality threshold or max-iters.
fit_for_business_ideation: Cheapest baseline — wrap any single-shot 'generate business idea' call in a Self-Refine loop with a critic prompt scoring novelty + feasibility + customer-pain-fit. 15-25% quality lift before any multi-agent infra.

title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
authors_or_maintainers: Shunyu Yao, Dian Yu, Jeffrey Zhao, Izhak Shafran, Thomas L. Griffiths, Yuan Cao, Karthik Narasimhan (Princeton/DeepMind)
summary: Generalizes Chain-of-Thought into a search tree: at each step, LLM proposes K candidate 'thoughts', a value/voter prompt scores each, BFS/DFS explores the tree. Game of 24: GPT-4 CoT 4% → ToT 74%. Includes a Creative Writing task.
applicable_pattern: Generate-evaluate-search at the thought level. At each node prompt for K candidates; score with a value prompt; expand best with BFS/DFS/beam. Backtracking is core — LLM can abandon a branch.
fit_for_business_ideation: Map business-idea space as a tree: root = problem space, level 1 = customer segments, level 2 = value props, level 3 = monetization. At each level expand K candidates, score, prune. Yields a portfolio with explicit reasoning paths.

title: Can LLMs Generate Novel Research Ideas? A Large-Scale Human Study with 100+ NLP Researchers
authors_or_maintainers: Chenglei Si, Diyi Yang, Tatsunori Hashimoto (Stanford)
summary: First rigorous head-to-head: 79 NLP experts blind-reviewed 49 ideas each from (a) expert humans, (b) an LLM ideation agent, (c) AI ideas reranked by a human. AI-generated ideas judged statistically more novel (p<0.05) than expert ideas, slightly weaker on feasibility. Recipe: retrieval over papers + over-generate-and-rank.
applicable_pattern: Retrieval-grounded over-generation + LLM-reranking. Retrieve ~100s of related items. Over-generate hundreds of candidates. Deduplicate. LLM-rerank via pairwise tournament. Optional human top-of-rank review.
fit_for_business_ideation: Essentially the architecture the user is building, validated. Substitute 'NLP papers' with 'concepts in your business KB.' Crucial empirical lesson: scale of generation + dedup + rerank matters more than fancy single-shot prompts.

title: AutoTRIZ: Automating Engineering Innovation with TRIZ and Large Language Models
authors_or_maintainers: Shuo Jiang, Weifeng Li, Yuping Qian, Yangjun Zhang, Jianxi Luo
summary: Encodes TRIZ's structured inventive-problem-solving methodology (40 inventive principles, contradiction matrix, ideality) as an LLM workflow. From a problem statement, the system identifies engineering contradictions, retrieves applicable inventive principles, and generates a solution report.
applicable_pattern: Structured-method-as-pipeline: encode a domain-expert framework (TRIZ, JTBD, Lean Canvas, Business Model Canvas) as a deterministic pipeline where each stage is a narrow LLM call. The framework provides scaffolding the LLM lacks.
fit_for_business_ideation: TRIZ's inventive principles map to business model patterns ('segment a service', 'invert customer/supplier relationship', 'merge with adjacent industry'). Build a 'business TRIZ' agent.

title: CrewAI
summary: Python framework for role-based multi-agent orchestration. Primitives: Agent (role + goal + backstory + tools), Task, Crew (sequential or hierarchical), Flow (event-driven state machine). No LangChain dependency. Built-in memory, 100+ tools.
applicable_pattern: Role-Goal-Backstory agent specification + manager agent for hierarchical delegation. Best for ideation pipelines mapping cleanly onto an org chart. Flows handle deterministic glue between LLM stages — keeps you Stella-compliant.
fit_for_business_ideation: Most natural framework for the user's project. Define ConceptRetriever, CombinationGenerator, MarketAnalyst, FeasibilityCritic, PitchWriter; chain them in a Crew. Backstory field injects persona diversity. Flow primitive holds deterministic retrieval/scoring code.

title: LangGraph
summary: Graph-based orchestration: nodes are agents/tools/functions, edges are control flow (including conditional and cyclic), state is a shared dataclass. First-class support for HITL checkpoints, persistence, streaming, time-travel debugging.
applicable_pattern: State-machine-of-agents with explicit edges. Best for workflows with loops or HITL approval gates. State is canonical and inspectable, making evals/replays tractable.
fit_for_business_ideation: Use when needing conditional routing ('if novelty < 0.6 → divergent agent; if feasibility < 0.5 → constraint-relaxer; else → output'). Checkpointing is the cheapest way to build a 'resume yesterday's session' UX.

title: AutoGen (now part of Microsoft Agent Framework)
summary: Conversation-as-orchestration framework. Primitives: ConversableAgent, AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent. Patterns: Two-Agent Chat, Sequential Chat, Group Chat (with GroupChatManager), Nested Chats. Original paper: arxiv:2308.08155.
applicable_pattern: Group Chat with a Manager: agents speak when the manager (or heuristic) selects them. Most flexible for free-form ideation — agents self-select speaking turns. Nested Chats hide a sub-team behind a single 'agent' interface.
fit_for_business_ideation: Closest analog to a brainstorm room. Set up Group Chat with personas (Visionary, Skeptic, Customer, Investor, Operator) and a Manager picking who speaks based on state. Nest a research subteam inside one agent.

title: Claude Agent SDK (Subagents + Skills)
summary: Runtime under Claude Code, exposed as a library. Subagents declared with description, system prompt, restricted toolset, optional model. Patterns: Operator/Orchestrator, Split-and-Merge (up to 10 subagents), Agent Teams with shared task list. Permissions/hooks system enforces safety.
applicable_pattern: Description-driven subagent invocation. The description is what the parent's LLM uses to decide routing — vague descriptions kill invocation. Subagents have isolated context (parent only sees final message).
fit_for_business_ideation: Native fit if user is in Claude ecosystem. Define subagents: concept-retriever (read-only KB tools), combinator (Sonnet, persona-cycling), critic (Opus), pitch-writer. Parent does Split-and-Merge for parallel concept-pair exploration.

title: smolagents
summary: Minimalist agent library (<1K LOC core). Distinctive feature: CodeAgent writes actions as executable Python rather than JSON tool-calls — empirically ~30% fewer steps and LLM calls. Supports any LLM via LiteLLM. Sandboxed execution (E2B/Modal/Docker/Pyodide).
applicable_pattern: Code-as-action agent. Instead of 'choose tool, fill JSON', the agent writes a Python snippet that may call multiple tools in one expression. For ideation: composes retrieval + filtering + ranking in one LLM call.
fit_for_business_ideation: Use when ideation involves heavy combinatorial work over the KB. Code agents express complex queries natively in one shot; tool-calling agents loop forever.

title: OpenAI Swarm / Agents SDK
summary: Educational framework demonstrating two minimalist primitives: Agent (instructions + tools) and Handoff (an Agent can return another Agent as the next responder). Stateless, client-side. Production users should migrate to the Agents SDK.
applicable_pattern: Handoff-as-routing. Each agent knows the small set it can hand off to and decides via tool-call when to delegate. No central orchestrator — control flow is emergent. Lightest-weight pattern.
fit_for_business_ideation: Useful for a 'scout' phase where a Triage agent classifies a seed prompt and hands off to one of (Consumer-Product, B2B-SaaS, Marketplace, Hardware) agents, each tuned with domain priors.

title: Multi-Persona Prompting for Design Concept Diversity
summary: Systematically tests persona-prompting strategies for design ideation. Finding: parallel personas (each with own prompt) > sequential personas > single prompt for diversity. Personas anchor generation in distinct semantic regions. De Bono's Six Thinking Hats adapts directly to LLM prompting.
applicable_pattern: Parallel persona fan-out: for each ideation step, run K personas (Six Hats, or domain-specific {customer, founder, investor, regulator, engineer}) in parallel, each producing one candidate. Dedup + merge.
fit_for_business_ideation: Cheap, high-leverage diversity intervention. Before any debate/critique pipeline, generate K initial ideas via K personas in parallel. Six Hats specifically: White=market data, Yellow=upside, Black=risks, Green=novel angles, Red=customer emotion, Blue=structuring discussion.

title: CreativityPrism: A Holistic Evaluation Framework for LLM Creativity
summary: Modern evaluation framework decomposing creativity into Quality, Novelty, and Diversity. Sub-metrics include originality, feasibility, fluency, flexibility (Guilford). Uses LLM-as-judge with semantic-entropy as a reference-free novelty/diversity metric. Companion: 'Rethinking Creativity Evaluation' (arxiv:2508.05470) for the critique of common pitfalls.
applicable_pattern: Multi-dimensional LLM-judge evaluation. Score (a) Novelty via semantic distance to KB embeddings + LLM-judge, (b) Surprise via inverse likelihood under base prompt, (c) Utility/Value via LLM-judge with rubric, (d) Diversity via pairwise semantic entropy across the slate. Use heterogeneous-judge ensembles.
fit_for_business_ideation: Missing eval layer most ideation systems skip. Build a CreativityPrism-style scorer: Novelty = 1 - max-cosine-sim to KB + LLM judge; Utility = LLM-judge against rubric (problem severity, willingness-to-pay, defensibility); Surprise = how often a base prompt would produce this idea (low = high surprise). Use both for ranking AND as the critic signal in self-refine loops.
